documents.description: Awarded contract notice on Contracts Finder

tender.title: SUPPORTED ACCOMMODATION AND FLOATING SUPPORT FOR INDIVIDUALS WHO ARE HOMELESS OR AT RISK OF BECOMING HOMELESS
tender.description:
The aim of the following Services is to give Service Users who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless, access to supported accommodation or support in order to stabilise their lives and gain independent living skills. In addition, the Service will provide stable, quality accommodation which enables tenants to sustain their own tenancy, access appropriate services, receive support and move on into permanent housing.

The contract will commence on 1st April 2020 for a period of 3 years with option to extend for an additional 2 years subjecting to performance and funding.

The tender is broken down into the following Lots:-

LOT 1 - Supported Accommodation - 24/7 support to clients with mental health and/or drug/alcohol misuse issues
LOT 2 - Floating Support - to provide support to develop a service users capacity to live independently
LOT 3 - Supported Accommodation - 24/7 support to rough sleepers, amputees, those at risk of death by misadventure and substance misuse related chronic ill health.

Additional information: Organisations wishing to register an interest and download the tender documentation, should apply via the NEPO Portal only.
www.procontract.due-north.com 

All completed documentation must be returned via the NEPO Portal Only and by no later than 2pm pm on Friday, 17th January 2020.

tender.title: Extra Care Housing Multiple Award and COVID 19 12 Month Extension
tender.description:
This award is for an integrated care and support service in each
of the 11 existing ECH schemes within Bristol, using a service model that
combines care and support to meet service users' needs flexibly whilst
continuing to enable and promote independent living. Contracts extended for 12 months due to COVID 19.

documents.description: Awarded contract notice on Contracts Finder

tender.title: Cheltenham Road Affordable Housing Scheme
tender.description: In progressing its agenda to provide more affordable homes for rent within the borough, Corby Borough Council (CBC) has secured a "greenfield" site on the periphery of the town, with subsequent Full Planning Permission (18/00365/REG3 dated 9th April 2019 - Amended 28th May 2019) for a mix of 18 new dwellings. The site came with both physical and ecological constraints, including; traversing main sewers, close proximity to the railway, the presence of Great Crested Newts and other amphibians / reptiles. As such, this project has experienced some delays. Nevertheless, the scheme has been designed around the physical constraints and CBC obtained a License from Natural England to clear the site, which was successfully completed in September 2019. The Council would like to use this development as an opportunity to "pilot" ultra-low energy homes to help tackle environmental concerns and fuel poverty within the Borough. Therefore, the Council now wishes to appoint a competent design & build contractor (ideally with experience of delivering ultra-low energy homes) to complete the detailed technical design and build these dwellings, including all associated works. To this end, it has prepared this single-stage fixed lump sum tender. In order to evaluate the cost benefit of these ultra-low energy homes, all Bidders are required to submit both a Primary Tender (Option 1) for the provision of ultra-low energy homes and a Secondary "alternative" Tender (Option 2) for homes that simply meet the minimum requirements of the current Building Regulations. It will be for Bidders to determine how they reduce the use of energy within their scheme (presumably using a combination of improvements to the building fabric and technology) and the extent of that reduction, while optimising the balance between energy savings and the resulting increases in installation costs.

tender.title: Youth Housing Hub Service
tender.description: Process to appointed a provider who will establish and run the Youth Housing Hub. The Hub will be an integrated service that coordinates and delivers homelessness prevention, assessments, housing advice and the allocation of housing and support to the target client group. The Hub partner will provide a suitable building and manage the hub service and co-located team, including council social workers and housing advisers. 3 year contract with option to extend for 2 periods each of 2 years.  Contract value relates to maximum value if all extensions exercised.
